name: Greek Tomato Sauce with Olive Oil
recipeInstructions: Note: Tomatoes should be peeled and chopped if fresh, or you may substitute 1 two-and-one-half pound can plum tomatoes, chopped. Heat the oil in a heavy saucepan, stirring in the onions until transparent. Add the garlic, then tomatoes, sugar, wine, salt, and pepper, and cinnamon (if desired). Season to taste, then allow the sauce to simmer gently for 30 minutes. Remove the cinnamon stick before serving hot. For a thicker sauce, add 1/4 cup tomato paste diluted with 1/4 cup water when adding the tomatoes. To achieve a smoother consistency, strain the sauce and discard seeds just before adding parsley and basil.
